sections.subtitle: Team Overview and League Standing
sections.text: Le Havre, currently sitting in 16th place with 31 points, face a daunting challenge as they host Olympique de Marseille, who are in 2nd place with an impressive 59 points in the Ligue 1 standings. This matchup at Stade Oceane in Le Havre pits a struggling side against one of the league's top contenders. Marseille's superior position suggests they are the clear favorites, while Le Havre will be desperate to climb away from the relegation zone.

sections.subtitle: Historical Head-to-Head
sections.text: In their last 5 encounters, Olympique de Marseille have dominated with 4 wins and 1 draw, leaving Le Havre without a victory. The most recent clash on January 5, 2025, saw Marseille thrash Le Havre 5-1 at home, with Mason Greenwood earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.3 Sofascore rating. Marseille also hold a streak of 14 matches without a loss against Le Havre in head-to-head competition, further underlining their historical superiority.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players to Watch
sections.text: For Olympique de Marseille, Mason Greenwood is a major threat with 18 goals this season, ranking him 2nd in the league for goals scored, alongside a Sofascore rating of 7.51, placing him 4th league-wide. Adrien Rabiot, another key figure, boasts a rating of 7.34 and recent form with 2 goals and 2 assists in the last 5 matches. Amine Gouiri, with 11 goals and 5 assists, is also in fine form, averaging a 7.56 rating over his last 5 games. On the Le Havre side, Abdoulaye Touré leads the attack with 8 goals and an average rating of 7.06, while Yanis Zouaoui stands out defensively with a 69.8% ground duels won rate, ranking 5th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Olympique de Marseille come into this game with a streak of 3 matches without a loss, showing strong recent form. Le Havre, however, have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 home matches, while Marseille have gone 8 games on the road without one. Both teams have seen over 2.5 goals in recent matches, with Le Havre at 5 out of 7 and Marseille at 6 out of 7. Additionally, both teams scoring has been a trend, occurring in 9 of Le Havre’s last 10 home games and 6 of Marseille’s last 7 away matches.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights and Tactical Notes
sections.text: Le Havre have struggled defensively, conceding 66 goals this season, the worst in the league, and ranking 16th for least goals conceded. Despite this, they excel in headers scored with 9, ranking 3rd in Ligue 1, and clearances per game with 28.125, ranking 1st. Marseille, on the other hand, are prolific late in games, with 29.55% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Le Havre also struggle with possession, averaging just 42.66% (15th in the league), which could be exploited by Marseille’s attacking prowess.

sections.subtitle: Key Battles and Missing Players
sections.text: One key matchup will be between Le Havre’s midfield, led by Abdoulaye Touré, and Marseille’s dynamic duo of Adrien Rabiot and Pierre-Emile Højbjerg, who brings a rating of 7.35 to the table. Marseille will miss Ismaël Bennacer, a midfielder with 2 assists and a 7.15 rating, which might slightly impact their depth. Le Havre have no reported absences, giving them a full squad to utilize players like Issa Soumaré, who has 4 goals and 3 assists, in their bid to cause an upset.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Referee Willy Delajod averages 4.0 yellow cards per game, and with Le Havre committing fouls at a high rate (359 total, ranking 17th for most fouls), cards are likely. Both teams have trends of less than 4.5 cards in recent matches, with Le Havre at 5 out of 6 and Marseille at 8 out of 10. Corners are expected to be limited, as both sides have a streak of less than 10.5 corners in recent games, with Le Havre at 7 out of 7 and Marseille at 5 out of 5, alongside Le Havre’s low average of 3.94 corners per game (15th in the league).
